--- title: "Ahmadzai, CV" toc: true toc-location: left --- ```{=html} ``` [PDF](cv-ahmadzai.pdf) ## Education __2019__: Ph.D. Development and Agricultural Economics, University of Nottingham, UK __2011__: M.S. Agricultural Economics, Purdue University, USA __2005__: B.S. Agronomy and Soil Sciences, Kabul University, Afghanistan
__Primary Research Interests__: Agricultural & Food Policy • Climate Change, Adaptation, & Agricultural Resilience • Agribusiness & Farm Economics • Food Security, Rural Livelihoods & Agrifood Systems • Applied Econometrics and Impact Evaluation ## Academic and Research Appointments __06/2022–06/2025__: Research Fellow, [Center for Research in Economic Development and International Trade (CREDIT)](https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/credit/), University of Nottingham, Nottingham, UK. __04/2019–04/2022__: Postdoctoral Research Fellow, [International Center for Biosaline Agriculture](https://www.biosaline.org/), Dubai, UAE. __02/2012–09/2015__: Senior Economist/Head of Monitoring, Evaluation & Learning (MEL), Department for International Development (DfID, now [FCDO](https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/foreign-commonwealth-development-office)), Kabul, Afghanistan. __03/2011–09/2015__: Assistant Professor (Teaching-focused, Part-time), [Department of Agricultural Economics and Extension](https://ku.edu.af/en/department-agricultural-economics-and-extension), Kabul University, Kabul, Afghanistan. __07/2011–02/2012__: Economist and Value-Chain Development Specialist, FLAG International Afghanistan (FIA), Kabul, Afghanistan. __03/2006–10/2007__: Teaching Assistant, Department of Agronomy and Soil Sciences, Kabul University, Kabul, Afghanistan. ## Fellowships and Scholarly Appointments __2025–2027__: [MESA Global Academy Scholar](https://mesaglobalacademy.org/hayatullah-ahmadzai/), Middle East Studies Association (MESA).
Awarded a one-year scholarship (2025–2026), competitively renewed for a second year (2026–2027); conducting original policy-relevant research and engaging with scholars on Middle East and North Africa issues. ## Publications ### Peer-reviewed Journal Articles __[Climate shocks, household food security and welfare in Afghanistan](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.foodpol.2025.102910)__

Ahmadzai, H., and Morrissey, O. 2025. _The Journal of Development Studies_, 61(3), 381–399. __[Tests for market failures and overcoming transaction costs barriers to market participation in Afghanistan](https://doi.org/10.1007/s40847-024-00364-2)__

Ahmadzai, H. 2022. _FAO and ICBA_. ### Working Papers __Security, Infrastructure, and Gender Gap in Rural Labor Markets: Evidence from Afghanistan's Repeated Cross-Sectional Survey__
Ahmadzai, H.
_CREDIT Research Paper; in progress_ ## Research Grants and Projects __2022–2024__: [IIE-SRF Afghanistan Emergency Initiative Fund](https://www.scholarrescuefund.org/), Institute of International Education (IIE), and [Cara (the Council for At-Risk Academics)](https://www.cara.ngo/). £86,000. __2021__: [Catalyzing Investments and Actions to Enhance Resilience Against Sand and Dust Storms in Agriculture](https://www.fao.org/land-water/land/sds/en/), Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO). __Principal Investigator__. $150,000. __2021__: Climate-Resilient and Gender-Inclusive Cropping Systems in Climate-Vulnerable Areas in Asia and the Pacific, Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO). __Co-Principal Investigator__ with Dr. R.K. Singh (ICBA). $178,441. __2021__: [Developing National Policy to Utilize Treated Sewage Effluent (TSE) and Biosolids in Controlled Environment Agriculture in the UAE](https://www.biosaline.org/projects/developing-national-policy-utilize-treated-sewage-effluent-and-biosolids-controlled), Ministry of Climate Change and Environment, UAE. __Co-Principal Investigator__ with Dr. Khalil Ammar (ICBA). $100,000. __2020__: [Adoption and Impact of Quinoa Among Farmers in Marginal Areas of the MENA Region](https://www.biosaline.org/projects/quinoa-marginal-environments-phase-i), International Center for Biosaline Agriculture (ICBA). __Principal Investigator__. $68,488. __2019__: [Improving Agricultural Resilience to Salinity and Climate Change through Development of Pro-Poor Technologies and Management Strategies (RESADE)](https://www.biosaline.org/projects/improving-agricultural-resilience-salinity-and-climate-change-through-development-and), IFAD and BADEA. __Co-Principal Investigator__ with Dr. R.K. Singh (ICBA). $7,000,000. __2019__: EXPO Live Phase II: Value Chain Development for Innovative Halophyte-Based Food Systems, International Center for Biosaline Agriculture (ICBA). __Co-Principal Investigator__ with Dr. Dionysia Angeliki Lyra (ICBA). $500,000. ## Courses Taught Assistant Professor (Part-time), [Department of Agricultural Economics and Extension](https://ku.edu.af/en/department-agricultural-economics-and-extension), Kabul University, Kabul, Afghanistan. __2011–2015__: [AgEcEx731](https://ku.edu.af/en/department-agricultural-economics-and-extension): Agricultural Production Economics (undergraduate, spring terms) __2011–2015__: [AgEcEx836](https://ku.edu.af/en/department-agricultural-economics-and-extension): Agricultural Finance (undergraduate, fall terms) __2012–2014__: [AgEcEx630](https://ku.edu.af/en/department-agricultural-economics-and-extension): Introductory Statistics & Econometrics (undergraduate, spring terms) __2011–2015__: [AgEcEx735](https://ku.edu.af/en/department-agricultural-economics-and-extension): Research Methods & Projects (undergraduate; seminar-based teaching and student research supervision) ## Selected Presentations ### Conference Presentations __2026__: ["Climate Shocks, Household Food Security, and Welfare in Afghanistan."](https://events.stanford.edu/event/climate-shocks) Stanford University (FSE, Center for South Asia, Abbasi Program in Islamic Studies), April 22, 2026. Ph.D. Conference, School of Economics, University of Nottingham, Nottingham, UK, November 12–16, 2016. [Slides](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KHySQD_8y7S1k5tXvSlhw4IbW_DFn3uC/view) ### Invited Talks __2021__: "Challenge and Opportunity of Quinoa for Food and Nutrition Security." Panellist with Warren T.K. Lee, chaired by Bo Zhou. FAO Regional Office for Asia and the Pacific (FAO-RAP), Bangkok, Thailand, November 7–8. __2021__: "The Economics of Quinoa Production: Opportunity for Marginal Farmers." Panellist with Dr. José Graziano da Silva. Global Forum on Innovations for Marginal Environments (GFIME), Dubai, UAE, November 20–21. ## Awards __2021__: Co-recipient, [Khalifa International Award for Date Palm and Agricultural Innovation (KIADPAI)](https://www.kiaai.ae/en), awarded as Co-PI of the [Project](https://www.biosaline.org/news/2022-03-14-12684): "From Desert Farm to Fork: Value Chain Development for Innovative Halophyte-Based Food Products." __2015__: PhD Scholarship Award, University of Nottingham and Islamic Development Bank (IsDB). __2007__: Graduate Scholarship Award, Purdue University and USAID-funded Afghan Merit Scholar Program (AMSP). ## Service and Professional Activities __2019–present__: [External Research Fellow](https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/credit/fellows/fellows.aspx), Centre for Research in Economic Development and International Trade (CREDIT), University of Nottingham, UK. __2019–present__: Member, Agribusiness Sub-network, Association of Agricultural Research Institutions in the Near East and North Africa (AARINENA). __2019–present__: Peer Reviewer for _The Journal of Development Studies_; _Food Policy_; _Sustainability_ (MDPI); _PLOS ONE_; _Review of Development Economics_; _Humanities and Social Sciences Communications_; _Agriculture_ (MDPI); _Cogent Economics and Finance_; _Journal of Social and Economic Development_. ## Media and Outreach __2022__: [“How COVID-19 Crisis Undermined MENA States' Food Security.”](https://www.arabnews.com/node/1971861/middle-east) _Arab News_, November 20, 2022. __2021__: [“Agriculture in Marginal Lands.”](https://encyclopedia.pub/15034) _Scholarly Community Encyclopedia_, September 2021. __2021__: [“New Study Reviews Status of Marginal Lands Worldwide.”](https://www.biosaline.org/news/2021-08-29-7471) ICBA News Article, August 29, 2021. __2021__: [“World's Indigenous Peoples to Bear Brunt of Climate Change, Warns UN Paper.”](https://www.biosaline.org/news/2021-04-25-7369) ICBA News Article, April 25, 2021. __2020__: [“ICBA's Quinoa Varieties Show Promise in Morocco.”](https://www.biosaline.org/news/2021-04-15-7397) ICBA News Article, April 15, 2020. ## Professional Development __2020__ Responsible Conduct in Research: Research, Ethics, and Compliance. [Collaborative Institutional Training Initiative (CITI)](https://about.citiprogram.org/course/responsible-conduct-of-research-refresher/). Completed online, March 24–April 4. __2013__ Leadership and Governance of Development Programs and Projects. [Crown Agents International](https://www.linkedin.com/company/crown-agents). Dubai, United Arab Emirates, November 11–15. __2012__ Management of Agriculture and Rural Development Projects. [Asian Institute of Technology (AIT)](https://extension.ait.ac.th/). Bangkok, Thailand, November 21–December 1. __2008__ Academic English and Cross-Cultural Training Program. [International Centre for Intercultural Communication (ICIC)](https://archives.indianapolis.iu.edu/communities/8b5d9309-eacf-4381-81b4-ffabbab0f57d). IUPUI, Indianapolis, IN, USA, October 29, 2007–May 30, 2008. ## Skills - __Economic & Policy Analysis:__ Impact evaluation, program evaluation, benefit-cost analysis, value-for-money analysis, cost-effectiveness analysis, project appraisal, feasibility studies, policy analysis and design, welfare and distributional analysis, economic valuation, decision analysis. - __Quantitative Methods:__ Applied econometrics, causal inference, quasi-experimental methods (IV, DiD, RDD, matching, discrete choice, panel data), experimental methods (RCTs, field experiments), survey design and sampling, household survey design and implementation, mixed-methods research. - __Project and Grant Management:__ Grant writing and proposal development, multi-partner project management, interdisciplinary collaboration, stakeholder engagement, capacity building and mentoring, technical and policy reporting, scientific writing, theory of change development, M&E framework design. - __Software and Tools:__ Stata, R, Python (Pandas, NumPy, Statsmodels, Seaborn, Matplotlib), Power BI, QGIS, Excel, Survey Solutions, Qualtrics, LaTeX, Markdown. ### Linguistic - English (fluent) - Pashto (native) - Dari/Persian (fluent) - Arabic (conversational)
